Motion For Prove Up Of Default Judgment
Off Calendar Oct-27-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing as to defendants Faith Y. Pai and Donald G. Levenson.
Dept. 514. Judge: Gerardo C. Sandoval. Clerk: Kevin Dougherty. Reporter: Giselle Casey, CSR #8098. Telephone: 415-551-3680. Email: gcasey@sftc.org.

Robert H. Staley, Esq. appeared for plaintiffs Welling Financial Group, LLC and Harry Singer Foundation, Inc.
Melvin Kaplan, Real Estate Investor and Managing Member of Wellington Financial Group, Inc., was placed under oath and testified.
No appearance by defendants Faith Y. Pai or Donald G. Levenson.
Plaintiff's Complaint for Breach of Contract was filed on July 25, 2019.
Substitute service of plaintiff's Summons, Complaint and accompanying documents on defendants Faith Y. Pai and Donald G. Levenson occurred on August 1, 2019, and was effectuated on August 11, 2019.
Default as to both defendants was entered on September 16, 2019.
Plaintiffs' Memorandum of Costs was filed on October 5, 2020.
DOE defendants were dismissed today, October 27, 2020.

Plaintiffs Wellington Financial Group, Inc., et al. submit the following exhibits for the Court's review:
Exhibit 1: Promissory Note dated March 19, 2029.......................................ID/EVID
Exhibit 2: Promissory Note dated April 9, 2019........................................ID/EVID

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of plaintiffs Wellington Financial Group, LLC and Howard Singer Foundation, Inc., and against the defaulted defendants Faith Y. Pai and Donald G. Levenson, Joint and Several, as follows:

Promissory Note dated March 19, 2019:
Damages/Principal - $503,927.16.
Prejudgment Interest - $81,006.00.
Attorney Fees - $10,160.00.
Costs - $1,641.85.

Total - $596,735.01.

Promissory Note dated April 9, 2019:
Damages/Principal - $60,000.00.
Prejudgment Interest: $9,315.81.
Attorney Fees - $10,160.00.
Costs - $1,641.85.

Total - $81,117.66.

Counsel for plaintiffs shall prepare the proposed Judgment and submit same to the Court for review and signature.

Notice Of Application And Hearing For Right To Attach Order And Writ Of Attachment For Wellington Financial Group, Llc
Off Calendar Oct-17-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Robert H. Staley, Esq. of Epstein, Englert, Staley & Coffey, T:415.398.6392, for plaintiff/moving party;
No other appearance.

RULING: There being no objection, the Court adopted the tentative ruling on PLAINTIFFS WELLINGTON FINANCIAL GROUP, LLC, HARRY SINGER FOUNDATION INC.'s Hearing For Right To Attach Order And Writ Of Attachment For Wellington Financial Group, LLC:

Plaintiff Wellington Financial Group, LLC's amended application for a right to attach order and order for issuance of writ of attachment is granted. No opposition filed. The Court notes that the proofs of service show that defendants were served with the summons and complaint by substitute service on August 1, 2019, and with the attachment papers on September 4, 2019.

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com
